commit.message:
Auto-reboot Pico W into BOOTSEL mode for headless flashing (#45)

* Initial plan

* fix: auto-reboot Pico W into BOOTSEL mode for headless flashing

Add force_bootsel() function to flash.sh that uses `picotool reboot -f -u`
to programmatically reboot running Pico W boards into BOOTSEL mode via the
USB vendor reset interface. This removes the need for someone to physically
press the BOOTSEL button, enabling reliable headless CI flashing.

Changes:
- Add force_bootsel() helper that reboots up to 2 running devices
- Call force_bootsel before flashing device 1 (initial preparation)
- Call force_bootsel before flashing device 2 if it fell out of BOOTSEL
- Replace manual "hold BOOTSEL" error message with CI-friendly guidance
- Remove "Please put the second Pico W into BOOTSEL mode now" prompt

commit.message:
feat: LLM filtering for harvest pipeline (#11)

Regex extraction catches too much junk (29 candidates, 8 real).
LLM verification step filters noise before writing.

engine/llm_filter.py:
  - 3-tier backend: OpenAI API → Copilot CLI subprocess → regex-only fallback
  - Batch processing (max 20 candidates per call)
  - filter_decisions(), filter_patterns(), filter_people()
  - JSON response parsing with fence-stripping
  - Fail-open on errors (never blocks harvest)

engine/mock_llm.py:
  - Deterministic MockLLMFilter with configurable pass_rate
  - Keyword overrides for test control

Flags: --llm-filter (default on), --no-llm-filter (CI/fast mode)
Dry-run shows filtered vs kept with reasons.

53 new tests, all passing. Closes #11.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Add crash dump capture and analysis to --debug-output (#414)

## Description
When a WinUI 3 app crashes during winapp run --debug-output, the
previous output only showed a bare exception code and address. This PR
adds automatic crash dump capture and in-process analysis using ClrMD
(Microsoft.Diagnostics.Runtime) — no external tools required.

### How it works

- Managed (.NET) crashes → analyzed with Microsoft.Diagnostics.Runtime
(ClrMD). No symbols needed, results are instant.
- Native (C++/WinRT) crashes → analyzed with system32\dbgeng.dll. Works
without symbols (shows Module+offset), but adding --symbols downloads
PDBs from Microsoft Symbol Server for full function names.

### Flow:
App crashes → Write dump → ClrMD analysis → Found managed exception? 
   → Yes: Show exception type, message, managed stack trace
   → No: Fall back to DbgEng native analysis (with or without symbols)
→ Terminal shows summary, full details in log file

### Output layering
Terminal: Crash summary: faulting symbol, module, source file:line,
compact stack (framework frames collapsed)
Log file: All OutputDebugString messages, first-chance exceptions, full
thread stacks with module names
Dump file: Full memory minidump for deep analysis with WinDbg or Visual
Studio
<!-- Briefly describe what this PR does and why -->

## Usage Example

<!-- If this PR adds or changes commands, flags, or APIs, include a
short code snippet -->
```bash
winapp run <output-folder> --debug-output
```

## AI Description

<!-- ai-description-start -->
This PR introduces crash dump capture and automated analysis during app
execution with the `--debug-output` flag. When a WinUI 3 app crashes,
the tool now automatically writes a minidump and performs a managed or
native analysis without needing external tools. Users can enrich native
crash analysis by adding the `--symbols` flag to download PDBs for
better diagnostics.

Usage example:
```bash
winapp run  --debug-output --symbols
```

## Summary

Moves preconnection slash discovery behind provider-owned ACP contracts
so shared runtime and UI code stop deciding which providers support
preconnection commands or how those commands are loaded. Copilot now
owns project-local `.agents` discovery, including flat `*.agent.md`
files whose invokable slash token always comes from the filename.

## What changed

1. Added `preconnectionSlashMode` to provider metadata and routed
`acp_list_preconnection_commands` through
`AgentProvider::list_preconnection_commands(...)` so shared backend code
only validates scope and dispatches.
2. Added neutral parsing and dedupe helpers in
`acp/preconnection_slash.rs`, then implemented provider-owned
preconnection loading for Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, OpenCode, and
Copilot.
3. Reworked the frontend warmup/composer flow to key off provider
metadata instead of `loadsRemotePreconnectionCommands`, and stopped
falling back to empty preconnection slash sources after connection.
4. Refreshed the provider-owned architecture solution doc with the
preconnection boundary and the Copilot filename-derived flat-agent token
rule.

## Provider modes

| Provider shape | Mode |
|--------|--------|
| Claude Code / Cursor / Codex | `startupGlobal` |
| OpenCode / Copilot | `projectScoped` |
| Shared runtime fallback | `unsupported` |

r600: rescope C-Track after 4-way rubber-duck consensus

C0 (GPR spill path) added as P0 correctness blocker — all 4 reviews agreed
spill fallback outranks scheduler refinements. C-IF (Mandate #13 bcsel cap)
added as P1 correctness guard. C4+C5 merged into one interleaved pass per
mandate #8. C5 weighted-sum replaced with lexicographic/gated priority
(PV/PS affinity > clause continuity > critical path > Sarkar > channel
diversity). C2 deferred unless C4+C5 reveals missing state. C3 becomes
verify/close against existing SFN barrier mechanisms. C6 already CLOSED.

Key findings adopted:
- 5-step cascade = legality (must always run), clause continuity = heuristic
  (preference among legal candidates) — different layers, don't conflate
- Topology rejection (84 events) >> physical rejection (6 events) — channel
  geometry is the dominant loss mode, not silicon legality
- CPU bottleneck (GPU 62-75% idle) means scheduler changes must be lightweight
- required_instr is inter-group only — insufficient for intra-group barriers
- Existing SFN mechanisms (AluGroup, readport validation, KCACHE reservation,
  PV/PS boost, clause boundary invalidation) are FULL — do not reimplement

commit.message:
Remove FprimeRouter Copy (#4984)

* Remove unnecessary buffer allocation/copy in FprimeRouter for file and unknown packets

Instead of allocating a new buffer and copying data for file and unknown
packet types, pass the original buffer directly to the receiver. The
buffer is not returned to the deframer via dataReturnOut until it comes
back on fileBufferReturnIn, at which point an empty context is
constructed for the return.

This eliminates the need for bufferAllocate and bufferDeallocate ports,
as well as the AllocationReason enum and AllocationError event.

Co-Authored-By: michael.d.starch <michael.d.starch@jpl.nasa.gov>

* Remove dangling fprimeRouter buffer allocation connections from subtopologies

Co-Authored-By: michael.d.starch <michael.d.starch@jpl.nasa.gov>

* Move dataReturnOut into each switch case instead of using early returns

Co-Authored-By: michael.d.starch <michael.d.starch@jpl.nasa.gov>

* Fix formatting: remove extra blank line

Co-Authored-By: michael.d.starch <michael.d.starch@jpl.nasa.gov>

* Update Svc/FprimeRouter/docs/sdd.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review: empty context on all return paths, clarify buffer return contract, document context discard

Co-Authored-By: michael.d.starch <michael.d.starch@jpl.nasa.gov>

* Clarify SDD: receivers must return buffers via fileBufferReturnIn

commit.message:
fix(orchestrator): critical timeout, validation, and default case

CRITICAL FIXES (Issue #1-3 from QA Review):

1. ✅ llm-gateway-client.ts: Add AbortController timeout (30s)
   - Prevents orchestrator hang if LLM gateway stalls
   - Adds try-catch on JSON.parse
   - Full Zod validation with 3 schemas (LLMMetrics, PlannerAction, PlannerGatewayResponse)
   - Handles AbortError explicitly

2. ✅ planner-map.ts: Add default case that throws error
   - BEFORE: Unknown tool silently returned null
   - NOW: Throws descriptive error with available tool names
   - Prevents silent failures in job queue

3. ✅ engine.ts: Fix undefined job.id type error
   - Added check for job.id before using it
   - Extracted jobId to variable for type safety
   - Applied non-null assertion for tenant_slug (validated earlier in function)

VALIDATION:
- ✅ npm run type-check: 11/11 packages successful
- ✅ npm run test --workspace=@intcloudsysops/orchestrator: 28 tests passing
- ✅ npm run test --workspace=@intcloudsysops/api: 162 tests passing
- ✅ Total: 190+ tests passing

PRODUCTION READY: All critical issues resolved. Safe to deploy to staging.
